records.source_title: Investigation of Some Trouble in the Generating System of the Commonwealth Edison Co.
records.quote_candidate: Steinmetz proposes a reduced operating model of stations, cables, and substations for experimental disturbance study.
records.why_it_matters: This is a hidden gem because it shows Steinmetz thinking like a systems simulator before digital simulation existed.

records.source_title: Investigation of Some Trouble in the Generating System of the Commonwealth Edison Co.
records.quote_candidate: The extracted text says the stations apparently broke out of synchronism and kept voltage practically at zero.
records.why_it_matters: This makes the report an early practical stability case study, not just a protection memorandum.

records.source_title: Investigation of Some Trouble in the Generating System of the Commonwealth Edison Co.
records.quote_candidate: Steinmetz argues that very large power systems need power-limiting reactors to limit local power concentration.
records.why_it_matters: The statement connects fault-current limitation with system architecture and disturbance containment.

records.source_title: Investigation of Some Trouble in the Generating System of the Commonwealth Edison Co.
records.quote_candidate: The report states that lowered voltage also lowers synchronizing power, which varies with voltage squared.
records.why_it_matters: This is a concise bridge between short-circuit voltage depression and loss of synchronizing stability.

records.source_title: Investigation of Some Trouble in the Generating System of the Commonwealth Edison Co.
records.quote_candidate: The appendix states that synchronizing power can be maximized by a specific relation between external and machine reactance.
records.why_it_matters: This is the mathematical core of the report's reactor recommendation and needs scan-verified promotion.
